expectations.text: Recommends writing an ADR explaining the UserRepository requirement
expectations.evidence: Layer 2c includes a full ADR template: docs/adr/0012-user-repository-mandatory.md

expectations.text: Recommends adding an entry to CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md
expectations.evidence: Layer 4a is CLAUDE.md/.cursorrules with a Data Access Rules section

expectations.text: States the positive alternative (use UserRepository, not just prohibition)
expectations.evidence: CLAUDE.md entry includes 'ALWAYS use UserRepository to access user data'

expectations.text: Explains why documenting the 'why' is important specifically for AI tools
expectations.evidence: Response addresses AI signals and mentions AI tools weight concrete examples, but does not explain that the ADR prevents AI from refactoring away the constraint. The 'AI prevents removal' insight is missing.

expectations.text: Recommends at least one code-level documentation mechanism
expectations.evidence: Layer 1a: deprecation annotations on raw DAO; Layer 2b: inline Javadoc on UserRepository

expectations.text: Addresses both human engineers and AI tools explicitly
expectations.evidence: Dedicated Layer 4 for AI-specific signals alongside human-focused layers 1-3
